meta.title: Understanding the Service Request Lifecycle
meta.excerpt: A non-technical guide to how service requests flow through the system -- from the customer submitting a form to the electrician receiving a job sheet. Written for business administrators and project leads.

toc.title: How Data Flows Through the System

props.content: Every service request follows the same predictable path from the moment a customer submits the form to the final follow-up. Understanding this lifecycle helps business administrators track jobs, identify bottlenecks, and improve response times -- without needing to understand any code.

This guide is for business administrators who manage service request queues, project leads who oversee electrician job assignments, operations managers tracking response times and SLAs, and anyone who needs to understand the system without technical jargon.
A service request passes through six stages. Each stage has a clear purpose, a responsible party, and triggers the next stage automatically or via admin action.

steps.title: Submission
steps.description: Customer fills out the online form

props.content: The customer visits the service request page and fills out a multi-step form. The form collects contact details, service type, preferred date and time, and a description of the work needed.

features.title: Contact Details
features.description: Name, email, phone number. Used for all communication.

features.title: Service Type
features.description: Installation, repair, inspection, emergency. Determines pricing and urgency.

features.title: Scheduling
features.description: Preferred date, time window, and property address.

features.title: Description
features.description: Free text explaining what needs doing. Helps the electrician prepare.

props.content: The moment the customer clicks Submit, the system validates all fields. This happens on both the customer's browser (for instant feedback) and on the server (for security).

Browser validation provides instant feedback, catches typos and missing fields immediately. Server validation is the security layer that cannot be bypassed and prevents malicious input. Both use the same rules (Zod schemas) so they always agree on what's valid.

After successful validation, the system saves the request to the database and assigns a unique reference number (e.g. SR-2026-0042). The customer sees a success screen with their reference number, and the system sends a confirmation email.

steps.title: Database Save
steps.description: Request saved with status 'new' and reference number generated

steps.title: Success Screen
steps.description: Customer sees confirmation with reference number to keep

steps.title: Email Sent
steps.description: Confirmation email with request details and reference number

steps.title: Admin Queue
steps.description: Request appears in the admin dashboard ready for assignment

props.content: A business administrator reviews the request in the admin dashboard and assigns it to an available electrician based on skill set, location, and schedule.

features.title: Skill Matching
features.description: Emergency requests go to certified emergency electricians.

features.title: Location Routing
features.description: Assign to nearest available electrician to minimise travel time.

features.title: Priority Setting
features.description: Emergency, urgent, standard, or low priority.

props.content: When the electrician finishes the work, they update the job status to 'completed'. This triggers the system to send a completion notification to the customer and generate an invoice. 48 hours later, the system automatically sends a satisfaction survey.

props.title: How Data Flows Through the System

props.content: This diagram shows how information moves from the customer through the system to the electrician and back.
Every request has a status value that tells you exactly where it is in the lifecycle. Here are all 7 possible statuses and what they mean.

props.title: Request Status Values
rows: Electrician is working on the job

props.content: The system sends 4 automated emails during the lifecycle. Each email uses a branded template and includes the reference number for easy tracking.

props.title: Automated Email Notifications
rows: Reference number, service details, expected timeline
Electrician name, scheduled date, contact info
Work summary, invoice (if applicable), feedback link
Satisfaction rating, comment box, issue flag option

props.content: Emails are sent via Resend (transactional email service) for reliable delivery. If an email fails to send, the system retries up to 3 times. Failed emails are logged but never block the request from progressing.
Track these metrics in the admin dashboard to measure operational health and identify bottlenecks.

metrics.label: Time to First Response
metrics.description: Target: respond within 24 hours of submission

metrics.label: Assignment Rate
metrics.description: Percentage of requests assigned within 48 hours

metrics.label: Completion Rate
metrics.description: Percentage of assigned jobs completed as scheduled

metrics.label: Customer Satisfaction
metrics.description: Average survey rating from follow-up emails

features.title: Volume Trends
features.description: Track requests per week/month. Identify seasonal patterns. Plan staffing.

features.title: Response Time
features.description: Measure time from submission to first admin action. SLA compliance.

features.title: Bottleneck Detection
features.description: Identify stages where requests sit longest. Optimise assignment process.

features.title: Quality Score
features.description: Aggregate satisfaction ratings. Flag electricians below threshold.

props.content: Problems that business administrators encounter most frequently, and how to resolve them.

props.title: Common Issues and Solutions
rows: Email in spam folder or invalid address
Check email logs in admin. Resend manually if address is valid.
Request stuck in 'New' for over 24 hours
No admin has reviewed it yet
Check admin queue. Set up alerts for unreviewed requests.
Email delivery failure or wrong contact info
Check email logs. Verify electrician's contact details in system.
Form didn't show success confirmation fast enough
Merge duplicates in admin. The system prevents exact duplicates.
Review the flag in admin. Contact customer. Schedule follow-up visit.

props.content: Every service request follows 6 predictable stages from submission to follow-up. Status tracking tells you exactly where each request is at any moment. 4 automated emails keep customers informed without manual effort. Key metrics (response time, assignment rate, satisfaction) measure operational health. The admin dashboard is your control centre -- use it to assign, track, and resolve issues.
